extraction.one_line_summary: GAVIRIA Fernando won from behind on downhill finish after POZZATO attacked solo with 3.5km remaining and BENNETT lost leadout train.
tactical_patterns: POZZATO attacked 3.5km out for leadout disruption not stage win, forcing rival trains to chase and fracture.
BENNETT's Bora leadout started too early on pan-flat stage, leaving sprinter isolated before final 600m turn.
GAVIRIA exploited downhill final 300m where comebacks typically fail, winning from worse position by holding momentum through descent.

rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: Fourth stage win in first Grand Tour at age 22 shows exceptional race maturity and finishing speed.
rider_signals.MORLAT Jakob: neutral: Second runner-up finish suggests consistent sprint speed but tactical positioning issues in final 200m.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: DNS after already being off GC pace indicates deeper fatigue/injury issues than Sky initially admitted.
rider_signals.POZZATO Filippo: neutral: Long-range spoiler attack 3.5km out demonstrates domestique willing to sacrifice for MORLAT positioning rather than pure showboating.
rider_signals.CALEB Ewan: negative: Lost leadout train and taken to barriers shows vulnerability in chaotic finishes despite having team support.
